_note: Seed list = INPUT to the compile workflow. The extract+verify agents read each source and produce ruleset.json. Rule DETAILS (thresholds, household-size tables) are intentionally NOT here - they are extracted from sources and adversarially verified. Marker '@verify' = URL/figure to confirm during extraction.

core.name: CalFresh (SNAP)
core.role: ANCHOR + master key. One enrollment categorically unlocks CARE/FERA, California LifeLine, Muni Lifeline, Recology, school meals. Also drives the PUMS impact stat (PUMS has a SNAP-receipt flag).
core.benefitSummary: ~$2,300/person/yr food benefit; CA est. ~$3.46B/yr left unclaimed @verify
core.ruleShape: gross income <=200% FPL (BBCE) + net <=100% FPL, by household size; many categorical paths. SCREEN, not final determination.

core.name: Medi-Cal
core.role: Second anchor; highest-value benefit (full health coverage). SSI auto-enrolls. Triggers CARE/LifeLine + WIC income test.
core.benefitSummary: Comprehensive coverage, $0 premium; open to all income-eligible adults regardless of status since 1/2024
core.ruleShape: income <=138% FPL (adults), <=266% (kids), <=213% (pregnant), by household size; categorical via SSI.

core.name: California LifeLine (phone / broadband)
core.role: Largest take-up gap of all (~77% of eligible NOT enrolled @verify) - highest marginal yield for a finder.
core.benefitSummary: Up to ~$19/mo voice/broadband (~$228/yr) + broadband add-on; 1 per household

core.name: CARE / FERA (energy discount)
core.role: Clean chaining demo (CalFresh -> CARE). Near-automatic add-on.

sources.note: PG&E CARE/FERA fact sheet @verify exact URL

core.name: Muni Lifeline / Clipper START
core.role: Keeps the tool visibly SF. Transit discount that chains off CalFresh/Medi-Cal.
core.benefitSummary: Lifeline pass ~$43/mo (~$516/yr saved); Clipper START ~50% off single fares
core.ruleShape: income <=200% FPL OR categorical (Medi-Cal/CalFresh/Unemployment via Clipper START).

sources.note: transitioning to Clipper START; no new Lifeline apps after 2026-05-01 @verify current path

core.name: Federal EITC + CalEITC (+ Young Child Tax Credit)
core.role: Cash headline (largest single payout). Does NOT chain - requires filing a return. Flag whenever a low-income earning household appears.
core.benefitSummary: Federal EITC up to ~$8,046; CalEITC up to ~$3,756; YCTC up to ~$1,189 (child <6) @verify TY
core.ruleShape: earned-income phase-in/out by family size; MUST file a tax return; SSN required (federal).

stretch.name: Recology Low-Income Discount
stretch.ruleShape: income <=200% FPL OR categorical; single-unit residential.

stretch.name: SF Universal Free School Meals
stretch.role: Universal (no income test) - simple, broad, kid-household relevance.
stretch.benefitSummary: Free meals for all SFUSD students (~$900-1,300/child value @verify)

stretch.name: SFPUC Customer Assistance Program (water/sewer)
stretch.role: Genuinely SF-built program; AMI-tier + account-type exclusions rule shape (good extraction variety).
stretch.benefitSummary: 25% off (<=50% AMI) or 40% off (<=30% AMI) water + sewer
stretch.ruleShape: income % of AMI tiers + residential single-family individually-metered account; not claimed as dependent.

stretch.name: WIC
stretch.role: Income test met by CalFresh/Medi-Cal for households with kids <5; then itself triggers CARE + LifeLine.
stretch.ruleShape: income <=185% FPL + nutrition risk; women + children <5. (nutrition-risk screen -> often partial)
